impact_on_program.next: Prove residual multi-mates are min-weight (pay/forbid long trades), then prove pairwise-tight (or size 1), OR attack U_phi.
impact_on_program.still_open: residual forbids long trades; residual tight fibers are cliques; or K_res=1; or U_phi bound

entropy.interpretation: Deployed free=846161 ≫ 1 so free-0/1 packing is unavailable. Average m-fiber size ~ 2^(-18820.25) ≪ 1, so random multi-mates are absurdly rare; residual uniqueness is still the expected truth but not proved. Min trade weight is {MIN_TRADE}.
entropy.name: deployed_avg_m_fiber

free_ge_2.name: free_ge_2_allows_long_trades
proof: cap = free − 1 ≥ 1 when free ≥ 2, so |∩| ≤ cap−1 is permitted by v9.
Toy suite certifies long trades for free ≥ 2 on (F_17, mu_16).
free_ge_2.statement: If free = m − w ≥ 2, then the intersection cap m−w−1 ≥ 1, so non-tight multi-mates with |C ∩ C'| ≤ m−w−2 (hence |△| ≥ 2(w+2)) are not ruled out by the intersection law. Toys realize such pairs; v8 coset pads with e0 > w+1 are long trades (|△| = 2 e0 > 2(w+1)).

free_le_1.name: free_le_1_only_min_weight_and_pack
proof: free=0: Phi_w determines the full monic locator ⇒ at most one m-set (v6).
free=1: cap = m−w−1 = 0, so |∩| ≤ 0 ⇒ disjoint multi-mates; difference is a nonzero constant (CS). Pack ≤ floor(n/m) (v6).
free_le_1.statement: If free = m − w = 0 (i.e. w ≥ m), then M_m^{max} ≤ 1. If free = 1 (w = m − 1), then every multi-mate pair is min-weight/tight (|△| = 2m, |∩| = 0), and M_m^{max} ≤ floor(n/m) by free-1 CS packing.

hypotheses_open: Residual multi-mates are all min-weight (no long trades in C_res)
Each residual multi-mate fiber is a pairwise-tight clique (not merely tight-graph connected)
min_weight_criterion.name: min_weight_pairwise_tight_gives_Kres_le_ktight
proof: Pairwise-tight family size ≤ k_tight (v9 tight-clique packing), applied inside C_res (v11).
min_weight_criterion.statement: If every residual multi-mate pair is min-weight (tight), and every Phi_w fiber of residual can-cores is a pairwise-tight family (equivalently: a tight clique), then M_m^{res,phi} ≤ k_tight = 18. If also U_res ≤ floor(target/(pack * k_tight)), then |R| ≤ target.
min_weight_criterion.warning: Tight-graph connectivity alone is insufficient: toys show tight components larger than k_tight when the component is not a clique.

program.name: v12_reduction

tight_component_warning.name: tight_graph_component_can_exceed_ktight
proof: Computational certificate in toy suite: e.g. (p,n,m,w)=(17,16,4,2) has a fiber of size 8 with max tight-component 8 > k_tight=5, and the fiber is not a tight clique (nontight edges present).
v9 packing applies only to families where EVERY pair is tight.
tight_component_warning.statement: Let G_tight be the graph on a Phi_w fiber with edges = tight multi-mate pairs. A connected component of G_tight can have size > k_tight. Therefore 'all multi-mates are tight-adjacent to something' does not imply M_m ≤ k_tight; one needs a pairwise-tight (clique) hypothesis.

classification.long_trade: |△| ≥ 2(w+2) = non-tight; class (ii) or (iii) by plant factor

trade_weight.name: multimate_trade_weight_law
proof: v9 intersection law: |C ∩ C'| ≤ m − w − 1 from deg(Λ_C − Λ_{C'}) ≤ m − w − 1 and C ∩ C' ⊂ roots of the difference.
Symmetric difference size identity: |C △ C'| = |C| + |C'| − 2|C ∩ C'| = 2m − 2|C ∩ C'|.
Equality iff |C ∩ C'| = m − w − 1 iff tight pair (v9 tight-pair law).
trade_weight.statement: Let C ≠ C' be m-subsets with Phi_w(C)=Phi_w(C'). Then |C ∩ C'| ≤ m − w − 1 and |C △ C'| = 2(m − |C ∩ C'|) ≥ 2(w + 1). Equality |C △ C'| = 2(w + 1) holds if and only if the pair is tight (|C ∩ C'| = m − w − 1), iff C,C' are free-1 CS pads of (w+1)-blocks on a common core of size m−(w+1) (v9).

title: Trade-weight law; free-regime packing; min-weight residual criterion
